samples.xml_description:
```xml
<module id="adder_pipe_64bit">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit ripple carry adder with pipelining stages.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="i_en">Enable signal for addition operation.</input>
        <input id="adda" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="addb" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[64:0]">65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb.</output>
        <output id="o_en">Output enable signal indicating the availability of the result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="IDLE" value="2'b00"/>
        <state name="STAGE1" value="2'b01"/>
        <state name="STAGE2" value="2'b10"/>
        <state name="DONE" value="2'b11"/>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="stage_reg" width="2" init_value="IDLE">Pipeline stage register.</reg>
        <reg id="result_reg" width="65" init_value="0">Result register.</reg>
        <reg id="o_en_reg" width="1" init_value="0">Output enable register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Pipeline stages
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                stage_reg <= IDLE;
                result_reg <= 0;
                o_en_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                case (stage_reg)
                    IDLE: begin
                        if (i_en) begin
                            stage_reg <= STAGE1;
                        end
                    end
                    STAGE1: begin
                        // Perform addition for first stage
                        result_reg[63:0] <= adda + addb;
                        stage_reg <= STAGE2;
                    end
                    STAGE2: begin
                        // Perform addition for second stage
                        result_reg[64] <= result_reg[63]; // Carry out
                        result_reg[63:0] <= result_reg[63:0] + result_reg[64];
                        stage_reg <= DONE;
                    end
                    DONE: begin
                        // Finalize result
                        result_reg <= result_reg;
                        o_en_reg <= 1;
                        stage_reg <= IDLE;
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Assign outputs
        assign result = result_reg;
        assign o_en = o_en_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>
```
